summary: Parotid duct traverses above or near surface of masseter before piercing into buccinator; no significant new evidence alters previous understanding about exact point where it exits superficial structures after passing superior/middle portion of mandible region. To be precise regarding exit location post-masseter passage would require more detailed imaging studies not currently available here but generally accepted as exiting at junction between deep facial muscles including those involved around mouth opening/closing actions triggered via motor innervation from cranial nerve VII/VIII complex controlling mastication/swallowing functions among others critical for oral health/digestion processes facilitated largely due proper functioning of these musculature systems interconnected closely throughout head/neck regions forming intricate network supporting overall physiology necessary sustaining life activities requiring coordinated effort amongst multiple organ/systems working together seamlessly under normal conditions barring any pathology disrupting natural balance inherent within such finely tuned biological machinery present even prenatally developing embryo stages onward until end-of-life senescence phase marking conclusion full circle back origin starting point cycle repeating endlessly unless interrupted prematurely by external/internal factors leading towards decline deterioration ultimately resulting death cessation all bodily function ceasing permanently once irreversible damage occurs beyond repair threshold reached indicating terminal stage approached nearing close chapter ending long journey begun birth
